reasoning: The base rate of a new vaccine being developed, approved, and added to a specific Wikipedia list page for a relatively obscure vector-borne disease in a span of weeks or months is extremely low. A 2022 article mentions "a promising lead towards vaccine development", which is pre-clinical research; actual vaccine approval takes many years. I place probabilities near the floor value of 0.02 for the first deadline, grading upward slightly to 0.05 for late Feb 2026 to reflect the minimal uncertainty in longer time horizons or Wikipedia edits mistakenly adding a vaccine name.
